title: cerasus avium
intro: Cerasus avium, also known as the wild cherry or bird cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects due to the presence of compounds such as salicylic acid and flavonoids. The health benefits of Cerasus avium are attributed to its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of the common cold and flu. Herbal preparations of Cerasus avium include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support respiratory health and relieve coughs.
